title: koniga halimifolia subsp. perusiana
intro: Koniga halimifolia subsp. perusiana, also known as Peruvian fleabane, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Koniga halimifolia subsp. perusiana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
